person.name: William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine

nameForms.fullText: William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir William Graham of Kincardine was the son of Sir Patrick Graham of Kincardine and of Dundaff and Matilda (?). He married Mariot Oliphant, daughter of Sir John Oliphant of Aberdalgy. He married, firstly, Gille Stewart, daughter of Sir John Stewart. He married, secondly, Lady Mary Stewart, daughter of Robert III Stewart, King of Scotland and Annabel Drummond, on 13 November 1413. He died in 1424. He lived at Kincardine, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. He was invested as a Knight. On 12 February 1407 he had a royal charter of an entail of the lands of Old Montrose.

«b»Children of Sir William Graham of Kincardine and Lady Mary Stewart«/b»
1.) Robert Graham

2.) Patrick Graham d. 1478

3.) William Graham ancestor to Graham's of Garvock.

4.) Walter Graham ancestor to Graham's of Knockdollan.

«b»Child of Sir William Graham of Kincardine and Gille Stewart«/b»
1.) Alexander Graham d. fr 14 Mar 1415/16-8 Jan 1421/22

«b»Children of Sir William Graham of Kincardine and Mariot Oliphant«/b»
1.) Elizabeth Graham

2.) John Graham

zh.lifeSummary: 當William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine於1368年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾布拉可福德時，他的父親Patrick Graham28歲，母親Matilda Maud Blackford16歲。他於1392年與Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie在英國蘇格蘭培爾金加迪結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1424年2月12日在蘇格蘭培爾金加迪享年56歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭斯林史爾斯特拉斯賀布藍。

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine was born in 1368, in Blackford, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Patrick Graham, was 28 and his mother, Matilda Maud Blackford, was 16. He married Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie in 1392, in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 12 February 1424, in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 56, and was buried in Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine est né en 1368, à Blackford, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Patrick Graham, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Matilda Maud Blackford, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie en 1392, à Kincardine, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 12 février 1424, à Kincardine, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine 1368 in Blackford, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Patrick Graham 28 und seine Mutter Matilda Maud Blackford 16. Er hat 1392 in Kincardine, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 12. Februar 1424 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine è nato il nel 1368 nel Blackford, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Patrick Graham, aveva 28 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Maud Blackford, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie nel 1392, nel Kincardine, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 12 febbraio 1424, nel Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardineが1368年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアブラクフォードで生まれた時，父Patrick Grahamは28歳，母Matilda Maud Blackfordは16歳であった。1392年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアキンカーディンでMariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellieと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1424年2月12日にスコットランドペルスシュアイアキンカーディンで56歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドスティルリングシュアイアストラスブランにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine(이)가 1368년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 블랙퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Patrick Graham(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Maud Blackford(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1392년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 킨카딘에서 Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1424년 2월 12일 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 킨카딘에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 스티를링실르 스트래스블랜에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine nasceu em 1368, em Blackford, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Patrick Graham, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Matilda Maud Blackford, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie em 1392, em Kincardine, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 12 de fevereiro de 1424, em Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine родился в 1368 г. в местности блэккфорд, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Patrick Graham было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda Maud Blackford, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie в 1392 г. в местности кинкардаин, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 12 февраля 1424 г. в местности кинкардаин, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности стратблан, стирлингшир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Graham 1st Lord Graham of Kincardine nació en 1368, en Blackford, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Patrick Graham, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Matilda Maud Blackford, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Mariota Oliphant Of Aberdalgir and Kellie en 1392, en Kincardine, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 12 de febrero de 1424, en Kincardine, Perthshire, Scotland, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Strathblane, Stirlingshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
